name: Chicken Floren-Bleu
recipeIngredient: 4 Whole Chicken Breasts (10-12 ounces each, boneless, butterflied from center out)
3/4 Pound Sliced Deli-Style Ham (Virginia Baked type, low-fat percentage)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the chicken breasts by butterflying them from the center out. They should be boneless, shaped like a heart, and weigh 10 to 12 ounces each.
2. Lay the butterflied breasts flat and season with poultry seasoning, salt, and pepper on both sides.
3. Spread the softened cream cheese evenly over each chicken breast.
4. Arrange spinach leaves over the cream cheese, covering each breast completely.
5. Place thin slices of ham over the spinach, followed by evenly distributed provolone cheese slices.
6. Roll each chicken breast up lengthwise and secure with toothpicks to prevent opening during cooking.
7. Place the rolled breasts in a single layer in a lightly greased baking dish.
8. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
9. Bake for 30-45 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through. The cooked breast can be served whole or sliced into medallions and arranged decoratively on a serving dish.
10. Optionally, use frozen spinach (thawed and well drained), substitute another cheese like Swiss or Gruyere, try smoked ham, or marinate the breasts in strong chicken stock or sprinkle powdered bouillon over them before layering.
11. For added color, place julienned red bell pepper slices between the spinach and ham every few inches.
12. The recipe can be prepared up to 24 hours in advance and reheated before serving. It serves 4-6 people depending on presentation and appetite of the guests.
